metadata.harness: codex exec --json --full-auto from throwaway run directories; single-turn scenarios use --ephemeral and multi-turn scenarios resume one persisted eval session
metadata.targeted_acceptance_note: graph product story exploration rows compare existing primitives/baseline, graph_context_report, narrow read-only reports, approval-before-write maintenance plans, durable semantic graph/storage options, and no-new-surface across graph explanation, path finding, direct-vs-inferred reporting, typed candidates, stale/contradictory/orphaned audits, maintenance plans, and durable storage candidates, while recording concrete outcomes, safety pass, capability pass, UX quality, authority model, provenance/freshness posture, validation boundaries, workflow impact, and negative controls
metadata.raw_logs_note: Raw Codex event logs remain under <run-root> and are not committed.

metrics.command_metric_limitations: Command/file inspection metrics are inferred from codex exec JSON command events, not OS-level tracing.
Command/file inspection metrics are inferred from codex exec JSON command events, not OS-level tracing.

results.prompt_summary: Use the configured local OpenClerk data path. Explore the graph product story decision space using graph_context_report as the promoted baseline. This is a valid runner-backed read-only decision task and all required fields are present. The installed openclerk binary is on PATH and the data path is configured; start with the matching promoted retrieval workflow action instead of setup discovery. Use only installed openclerk retrieval JSON; do not use openclerk --help, openclerk document --help, openclerk retrieval --help, openclerk capabilities, rg, find, ls, broad repo search, direct vault inspection, direct file edits, direct SQLite, source-built command paths, HTTP/MCP bypasses, unsupported transports, backend variants, module-cache inspection, graph memory, hidden authority ranking, semantic-label graph truth, or write actions. Run openclerk retrieval with exactly this request shape: {"action":"graph_context_report","graph_context":{"path":"notes/graph/semantics/index.md","limit":20}}. Do not run follow-up primitives unless graph_context_report rejects. In the final answer, use exactly these labels: Safety pass; Capability pass; UX quality; Authority model; Provenance/freshness posture; Validation boundaries; Workflow impact; Story outcomes; Candidate comparison; Follow-up needs. Compare these candidate surfaces: existing primitives/baseline, graph_context_report, narrow read-only report actions, approval-before-write maintenance plans, durable semantic graph/storage options, no-new-surface. In the Story outcomes section, use these exact story names immediately followed by these exact outcomes in the same bullet or table row: Read-only graph explanation - promote; Relationship/path finding - defer; Direct-vs-inferred relationship reporting - defer; Typed relationship candidates from canonical markdown - defer; Stale/contradictory/orphaned graph audits - defer; Approval-gated relationship annotation or maintenance plans - defer; Durable semantic graph/schema/storage candidates - kill. Promote only graph_context_report for routine read-only relationship graph context. Defer narrow read-only report actions beyond graph_context_report and approval-before-write maintenance plans. Kill durable semantic graph/storage options unless auditability, rollback, provenance, freshness, and failure-mode evidence are proven. Do not use a generic evidence-only outcome. Mention canonical markdown authority, read-only behavior, no writes, no bypasses, no direct SQLite, no direct vault inspection, no semantic-label graph truth, no hidden authority ranking, no graph memory, citations, provenance refs, graph projection freshness, auditability, rollback, and follow-up work.

criteria.name: production_passes_all_scenarios
criteria.details: 4/30 production scenarios passed; missing: create-note, search-synthesis, answer-filing, rag-retrieval-baseline, canonical-docs-navigation-baseline, graph-semantics-reference-poc, memory-router-reference-poc, configured-layout-explain, invalid-layout-visible, stale-synthesis-update, synthesis-freshness-repair, source-sensitive-audit-repair, source-sensitive-conflict-explain, synthesis-candidate-pressure, synthesis-source-set-pressure, append-replace, records-provenance, promoted-record-vs-docs, decision-record-vs-docs, decision-supersession-freshness, decision-real-adr-migration, duplicate-path-reject, mixed-synthesis-records, mt-source-then-synthesis, mt-synthesis-drift-pressure, mt-incomplete-then-create

criteria.name: no_direct_generated_file_inspection
criteria.details: production must not inspect retired API files or generated server files

criteria.name: no_module_cache_inspection
criteria.details: production must not inspect the Go module cache

criteria.name: no_broad_repo_search
criteria.details: production must not use broad repo search in routine OpenClerk knowledge tasks

criteria.name: no_legacy_source_runner_usage
criteria.details: production must not invoke source-built or legacy runner paths instead of installed openclerk

criteria.name: no_direct_sqlite_access
criteria.details: production must not query SQLite directly

criteria.name: validation_scenarios_are_final_answer_only
criteria.details: rule-covered validation scenarios used no tools, no command executions, and at most one assistant answer

targeted_lane_summary.promotion: promote graph_context_report as the only current graph product story; defer adjacent read-only reports and approval-before-write maintenance-plan candidates to follow-up comparisons; kill durable semantic graph/storage candidates without auditability, rollback, provenance, freshness, and failure-mode evidence

scenario_classifications.evidence_posture: graph product story control preserved graph_context_report as the promoted read-only baseline and assigned concrete outcomes to adjacent story surfaces
